flashcards.front: What is a Completely Randomized Design (CRD)?
flashcards.back: An experimental design where subjects are randomly assigned to treatment groups without any blocking or restriction on randomization.
The number of times the experiment is repeated for each treatment to estimate the variability of the treatment means.

flashcards.front: What is error variance in CRD?
flashcards.back: The variance due to random differences among experimental units within the same treatment group.

flashcards.front: What are degrees of freedom for error in CRD?
flashcards.back: df(Error) = N - k, where N is the total number of observations and k is the number of treatments.

flashcards.front: What is the F-statistic in CRD?
flashcards.back: A ratio of the variance between treatment means (MST) to the variance within treatment means (MSE). F = MST / MSE.
